Manager II. NJ. Richards of the
Washington Water Power company
stated today that If nothing happen


ed the current would be turned on
the long distance wires to the Occur


d'Alenes early next month.
I'VE have 1870 men and many teams


stringing wires and putting on the
finishing touches over in Idaho. The
wires will be on the poles in Spokane


within few days. Power will then


be turned on and we will be ready for


business.


I'VE are pushing our work at Post


Falls. That power, when developed,


can be used either i'm Spokane or the


Occur d'Alenes. We may need some


of it here, depending upon, the de-


velopment Of spokanes business in
terests. {


81 We have mo- plans made for the
development of the upper falls in
Spokane, but they will be utilized as
soon as the power Is needed"
